title: Covering a cake with fondant
subtitle: Rolling out ready-to-roll icing and applying it smoothly
excerpt: Ready-to-roll fondant gives a porcelain finish. The method is rolling, lifting, draping, and smoothing, with a cake smoother to work out any creases or air bubbles. The cake must have a firm, dry base coat of either buttercream or marzipan before the fondant goes on.
sourceNotes: Ready-to-roll fondant (fondant plasticine or sugarpaste) has been a domestic decorating staple since the mid-twentieth century. The technique of rolling fondant in a sheet and draping it over a cake is documented in British domestic cake-decorating guides from the 1970s onward and is now fully public-domain practice described in household baking references. The underlying principles of sugar-based plastic icings are traceable to confectionery texts from the nineteenth century, including Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136, where moulded sugar pastes are described in detail.

content.text: Fondant covering requires the cake to have a firm, slightly sticky base layer. Buttercream that has been chilled for 20 minutes works well; marzipan brushed with a little water works equally well. The fondant clings to a tacky surface and slides on a dry one.

Knead the {{fondant-icing}} until it is supple and smooth. If it cracks when folded, it is too dry; knead in a few drops of water. Dust the work surface lightly with {{icing-sugar}}. Roll the fondant to 4 to 5 mm thickness, rotating the sheet a quarter turn every few rolls to keep it even and prevent it from sticking. Aim for a circle about 40 cm across for a 20 cm cake.

Lift the fondant over the rolling pin and drape it centrally over the cake. Press the top surface gently with flat hands, working outward from the centre to push out any air. Use the cake smoother in circular strokes to finish the top.
Press the fondant against the sides of the cake with flat hands, working downward.

will form at intervals; deal with each pleat before moving on. Once the sides are smooth, run the cake smoother firmly around the sides for a polished finish. Trim the excess at the base with a sharp knife or pizza cutter, holding it flush against the cake board.
